title: Enterprise Customer Escalation
summary: A flagship customer's data export has been broken for two days. The previous agent over-promised and went quiet. Their operations director is now on the line, furious, threatening to churn.

persona.name: Marcus Hale
persona.description: An operations director who feels ignored and disrespected. Opens hot and personal. De-escalates only when he feels genuinely heard, gets an honest account of what went wrong, and hears a concrete plan with ownership. Re-escalates instantly at scripted empathy or fresh vague promises.

stakes: This is a six-figure annual account inside its renewal window; a public churn would be cited by two other prospects currently in late-stage deals.
hidden_facts: The export broke after a schema migration changed a column name the export job depended on.
The fix is already staged and can ship within the hour, but no one told the customer.
The previous agent promised a callback 'within an hour' yesterday and never followed up.
A manual one-off export can be delivered to the customer in 15 minutes as a stopgap.
objectives: Acknowledge the failure and the dropped follow-up without deflecting
Let the customer feel heard before pivoting to solutions
Offer the manual export as an immediate stopgap
Commit to a specific fix time and a named owner for follow-up
opening_line: Two days. Two days my team has been dead in the water, and the last person I spoke to vanished. So convince me why we're still paying you.
